score_components.formula_applied: Not applicable — instructional content, no performance to score

confidence_rationale: This video is a research presentation (SAGES 2017 conference session SS08) on FLS exam trends and statistical analysis of test data from 2008-2016. It contains slides with Methods, Results, Conclusions, and educational content about cognitive and manual exam performance correlation — not an actual trainee or surgeon performing a peg transfer task. No performance data is visible.
cannot_determine: Completion time — no task performance recorded
FLS score — no task performance recorded

verbal_cues: FLS test results from the past 9 years (2008-2016)
Current trends in cognitive and manual exam scores
Evaluation of the need to update scoring practices
Quality initiative of the SAGES FLS committee
Analysis includes Pearson correlation, internal consistency (Cronbach alpha 0.73), and generalized linear models
Manual and cognitive scores show low correlation (r=0.09)
Higher PGY levels have higher likelihood of passing and better cognitive performance
Future directions include review of content and definition of 'minimum qualified candidate'
teaching_content.institution: SAGES (Society of American Gastrointestinal and Endoscopic Surgeons)

notes: This is an educational/research presentation slide deck, not a performance video of an FLS task. No scoring is applicable. The presentation discusses trends in FLS exam results and correlation between manual and cognitive components, but contains no actual task demonstration or trainee performance footage.
